prompt:
Vertical 9:16 cinematic devotional oil painting video clip, slower pacing with longer holds and restrained cuts.

Classical devotional oil painting realism. A faint break of pale light begins to touch a thorn-crowned brow as the dark recedes; visible brushwork, craquelure, aged varnish throughout.

Scene contains:
A close view of the crucified Christ's face turned gently upward, the crown of thorns above, the anguish softening as a faint break of pale light begins to touch the brow against the receding dark, a single tear on the cheek, deep shadow still at the edges. The scene is a painted tableau — a frozen moment, not an action. Nothing moves; this is a still painting.

Scene:
Start on full composition — the gently upturned face beneath the crown.

Cut to close-up — the softened, lifted eyes.

Cut to insert — the first break of pale light on the brow.

Cut to insert — a single tear on the cheek.

Cut to macro detail — the crown of thorns catching the new light.

Cut to mid framing — the whole softening face.

Return to full composition.

End hold on the gently upturned face in the first light.

Micro-motion:
- minimal

Camera:
- crop-based reframing only
- no invented movement

Emotion:
The first turn from darkness to grace.

No added elements.

10.0s

9:16
negative_prompt: no camera invention, no zoom invented, no pan invented, no push-in invented, no dolly, no tracking, no rotation, no animated subject motion, no figures moving, no morphing, no scene morphing, no facial morphing, no glow added, no halo added, no light rays added beyond what is painted, no atmospheric haze, no scene enhancement, no divine effects added, no smooth transitions, no motion blur, no reinterpretation, no new elements, no text, no watermarks, no extra limbs, no flickering, no warping, no animation, no action, no subject performs any task, no body motion, no limbs moving, the painting is fixed and frozen, only the camera reframes within the still image

beats.description: Start on full composition — gently upturned face beneath the crown
Cut to close-up — the softened, lifted eyes
Cut to insert — the first break of pale light on the brow
Cut to insert — a single tear on the cheek
Cut to macro detail — the crown of thorns catching the new light
Cut to mid framing — the whole softening face
Return to full composition, end hold on the gently upturned face in the first light
